rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF AMALIA PULE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #2, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/03/2021 to be heard with the related petition. The following issues must be addressed: 1) No contact information is on file. 2) No status report is on file. 3) Proof of payment of assessment must be filed once the assigned court investigator's report has been filed. 4) Paragraph 3 of the petition misstates the name and number of the related case. 5) Paragraph 7c: The total hours do not match the sum of subtotal hours. 6) Paragraph 8 and Exhibits A & B do not state whether the paralegal acted under the direction and supervision of an attorney. CRC 7.703(e)(1)(A) & 7.754. A statement addressing CRC 7.703(e)(5) is also missing. 7) No amended letters have been issued to reflect the corrected name of Fred Ochoa. The name on the bond has not been corrected either.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DEBORAH WHALLEY-KENDRICK
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/16/21. No courtesy (endorsed filed) copy of the supplement filed on 12/21/20, the day before the last hearing, has been received by the Examiner. [LRSF 14.7 B. and C.] The third supplement filed on 1/12/21 did not merely supplement (provide additional information); it amended the prior pleadings (materially changed information). [CRC 7.3.] An amendment requires notice. [CRC 7.53(a).] Furthermore, paragraph 2 claims that the Supplemental #2 I&A filed "concurrently" has an appraised amount of $74,030.45. No Supplemental #2 I&A was filed "concurrently" with that third supplement. The register of actions reflects a Corrected Final I&A filed on 1/15/21, showing an appraised amount of $73,970.45. The second paragraph 2 of the third supplement shows the proposed distribution, but that proposed distribution reflects statutory compensation that differs from the amounts shown in paragraph 3 (which should have been paragraph 4). That paragraph 3 of the third supplement also refers to paragraph 10 of the petition, which paragraph 10 mentioned that four I&As had been filed. Finally, without a full accounting, the Court cannot find that the gains and losses on sale are correct, as it appears that the administrator continued to reinvest or even purchase securities, not preserve or immediately liquidate them. Without a full accounting reporting the assets that were purchased on an informational schedule, the Court cannot confirm the assets that were sold or remain on hand. (It is noted that the sole heir waived an accounting.) It appears that a proper amendment to the petition (or a fully amended petition) should be filed to address all of the issues in one place. Notice must be given of the amendment (or amended petition) after it is filed. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/5/21. If the amendment (or fully amended petition) cannot be filed before 2/1/21 in order to give timely notice of the hearing on 2/16/21, Counsel should contact the Examiner to request a further continuance.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF CHUCK MING NGOW YEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. No proposed order or proof of service was received by the assigned Examiner by 1/15/21, as noted in the tentative ruling posted for the hearing on 12/29/20. (Proof of service was just filed on 1/20/21.) The petition does not include Haley Marie Ngow, who was shown in the initial petition for probate as a trust beneficiary and a minor. Notice must be given to a minor and also to the person having legal custody of the minor with whom the minor resides. [CRC 7.51(d).] (There is no need to disclose in this estate the dispositive provisions of the devisee trust.) (If there were an estate, the disclosure required to be made under Probate Code Section 9202(b) would have had to have addressed whether or not any heir or beneficiary had previously been so confined, not just that none is currently confined. Rev. and Tax. Code Section 19513 was repealed in 2018. The remedy for not having given timely notice is simply to request additional time in order to give notice, not file an amended petition.) A courtesy copy of the proof of service filed on 1/20/21, the verified supplement to this amended petition simply adding the minor trust beneficiary to the list of trust beneficiaries (if she is, in fact, a trust beneficiary), and proof of service of notice to the minor trust beneficiary and to the person having legal custody of her with whom she resides, and a proposed order must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/11/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF SUANNE L. CHAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/16/2021 Issues: 1) The petition for final distribution must state the date on which notice to the Franchise Tax Board was mailed pursuant to Probate Code 9202(c). [LRSF 14.36 L. 2)] 2) The petition alleges administrator's counsel will not seek an order for compensation. Please indicate if administrator's counsel is waiving compensation or will be paid outside of the estate. If attorney will be paid outside of the estate please indicate the amount as it cannot exceed the statutory amount. Please indicate if the attorney has already been paid. If the attorney had already been paid see CRC 7.700. The personal representative must neither pay nor receive, and the attorney for the personal representative must not receive, statutory commissions or fees or fees for extraordinary services in advance of an order of the court authorizing their payment.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE SHOSTAC LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 02/16/2021 for a court investigator's report on the related conservatorship to be completed. The following issues remain unaddressed: 1) The beginning balance of each account reported on Schedule A and corrected inventory and appraisal must be reconciled to the bank statements filed on 12/29/2020. 2) No statement of the First Republic Bank savings account has been filed. Although a one-page document titled "Savings Account History" has been filed, no explanation has been provided as to why an original statement similar to the checking account statement is unavailable for the savings account. 3) The First Republic Bank checking account statement shows interest payment of $4.67 on 01/10/2019, but Schedule B reported a different amount of interest on that date. 4) The First Republic Bank savings account history shows interest payment of $49.67 on 03/31/2019, but Schedule B reported no interest payment on that date.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF AUDREY J. LOOSER
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. The proof of service filed on 10/2/20 is incomplete at item 4a. Probate Code Section 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary was previously so confined, not just currently. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] (It is unclear if the proposed distribution reflects the deduction for attorney statutory fees. And if the administrator retained the E*Trade account, the assets on hand are presumably not all cash.) The waiver of account by Ava Pahlavan attached to the courtesy copy was not signed. It appears that a signed version was attached to the original. Is the courtesy copy of the petition a true and correct copy of the petition? (The signature also does not appear to be the same as the typed name... does she go by an alias?) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/10/21. NB: Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee WAS appointed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF VERNON W. HILLEND

rulings.Rulings: OFF CALENDAR. Petition for final distribution has been filed.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF DAWN LYNETTE NELSON-NOYES
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. Notice must also be given to the Director of Health Care Services, if the decedent was the surviving spouse of a person who received Medi-Cal benefits. [Probate Code Section 9202(a).] The petition fails to disclose whether or not notice was required by, or given pursuant to, Probate Code Section 9202(b). A petition for approval of an account or a report accompanying the petition shall contain all the statements required by Probate Code Section 1064(a). Did the fiduciary have a family or affiliate relationship with any agent he hired during administration? A courtesy copy of the supplement filed on 1/13/21 was not received by the Examiner by 1/19/21, as required in the preliminary tentative ruling posted on 1/14/21, but it appears that it should have been an AMENDMENT, as the attorney costs were increased (and still not set forth in detail with filing fees separated from filing service costs). [LRSF 14.7 B. and C. and CRC 7.3.] It is also assumed that the amount proposed to be distributed is the net amount after deducting the fees and costs. However, since the administrator is the sole remaining heir by virtue of his siblings' disclaimers (neither sibling reportedly having any issue who would take) and he has signed and verified the pleadings, these procedural issues are merely being pointed out and do not necessarily require any further curt response. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address the missing disclosures first listed above, however, must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/10/21. NB: In order for this Court to track closed matters, orders to terminate a proceeding must begin with the following language: "Order for Final Distribution," or "Order Terminating Conservatorship [or Guardianship or Trust]". [LRSF 14.36 I.6.b.] Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).] The first page of each filed paper must show the name and capacity of the party for whom the attorney is appearing. [LRSF 14.15 A.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE LEDGER 2004 REVOCABLE TRUST, DATED AUGUST 24, 2004
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Determining That The Amendment And Restatement Of Trust Is Invalid, Breach Of Contract, To Determine The Validity Of Provisions Of
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Remote appearance by video or telephone should be made whenever possible due to the recent stay at home order issued by the mayor. Please review the 8th amended general order for more information. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their full name. ISSUES (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Court to determine how to proceed. 2) Petitioner did not file the mandatory Notice of Hearing judicial council form for the First Amended Petition or Amendment to List of Exhibits. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing as the pleading it amends. (CRC 7.53). 3) Respondent has not filed a response or objections to the First Amended Petition.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF THOMAS IVELI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #1, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/1/21 to be heard by Judge Moody. The second amended petition for approval of the first and final account of the temporary co-conservators of the estate filed on 12/14/20 is still not procedurally ready to be heard. The second amended Final I&A filed on 11/23/20 still does not support the starting value in the second amended accounting. No proof of service of notice of how to object to the second amended I&A has been filed yet. The second amended I&A shows the appraisal date as 12/2/19, but the temporary co-conservators were appointed on 11/14/19. Original bank statements have not yet been filed. (They should be filed in the confidential file.) Some original statements sent via FedEx to the Examiner are awaiting a SASE for their return so that they can be filed with a cover sheet marked Confidential. (Alternately, Counsel could mail a cover sheet marked "Confidential" to the Examiner to foward to the clerk to file the statements.) The second amended petition is also incomplete: no exhibits are attached. It appears that a third amended accounting petition will need to be filed.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARITAL TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition Confirming Appointment Of Successor Trustees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Hearing issues have been e-mailed to counsel. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall. Please review the Court's 8th Amended General Order which took effect on 12/07/2020 for more information.

Matter has been continued to provide required 30 days notice. (Probate Code 851). 2) Please file a copy of the current deed for 239 Farallones Street. 3) A petition involving a trust must state the last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. (CRC 7.902). 4) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.93).

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF THOMAS FOON LEE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/21. A proposed personal representative is required to sign and verify all of his own pleadings, including any supplement or amendment thereto. [CRC 7.103(c).] At the very least, a waiver of notice by Sebastian Lee must be filed as notice to him was sent to the wrong address. (Item 3g(2)(c) of the petition should be answered when a petitioner is related to the decedent.) Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 2/10/21.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HAROLD LEVINE
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 2/23/2021. Issues: 1) A hard copy of the proposed order must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. (LRSF 14.7). [Proposed orders may not be e-filed.] Petitioner must submit a proposed order to the probate department at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. Failure to submit the proposed order at least five (5) court days in advance may result in a continuance for at least two (2) weeks. The proposed order must be a hard copy and prepared on the assumption that the petition will be granted. [LRSF 14.7] 2) Petitioner has not filed proof of publication. 3) A proposed nonresident personal representative will be required to post a bond to protect California creditors, even if the will waives, or all beneficiaries waive, bond. A declaration or attachment to the petition setting forth in detail the anticipated liabilities of the decedent and claims against the estate will be used by the Court to determine the amount of the bond, but in no event will the bond be less than $20,000. [LRSF 14.16 F.] 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form.
